description: Cross-registry resolution MUST refuse non-HTTPS schemes without attempting connection. The well-known URL is constructed as https://<authority>/.well-known/acdp.json (RFC-ACDP-0006 §4.1 step 2); a referenced authority that, by configuration override or DNS record, would force a downgrade to plain http:// MUST be rejected at the URL-construction stage.

input.scenario: Resolver is asked to dereference an acdp:// reference whose authority would resolve over plain HTTP

expected.behavior: Resolver MUST NOT open a TCP connection. Refusal happens before any DNS or socket activity directed at the upstream. plain HTTP, file://, ftp://, ws:// and any other non-https:// scheme MUST be refused identically.
expected.rationale: Plain-HTTP transport allows on-path attackers to substitute the capabilities document or DID document and defeats the registry-DID verification of §4.1 step 3.
